what_it_does: This noticeboard item introduces a research and architecture note for a fork, stating that live chat must use the channel fabric to inject messages into a live member's session and return replies without polling, and that it contains verified research, a clean architecture, and one real blocker.

purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a research and architecture note for the fork; the file actually states high-level requirements and intent, leaving the research details, architecture, and blocker unresolved.
apparent_themes: Live messaging over a channel substrate
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a design direction for real-time chat built on an existing channel system, rejecting save-and-poll patterns in favor of active session injection, and to alert readers that a blocking issue was discovered during research.
novelty: The chat is framed as active injection into a live member's session rather than a persisted drop-box, with 'no polling' stated as a hard constraint.
contribution: Contributes a concise problem framing and design constraint for a forked chat feature: messages must be injected into live sessions, replies delivered back, and polling avoided; it also signals that a blocker was identified and needs resolution.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item R0 defines a live chat requirement for a fork to deliver messages by injecting them into active member sessions over a channel fabric without polling, and notes an unresolved blocker.
intention_for_embedding: To set the architectural stance and constraints for implementing live chat on the channel system in a fork.
